input:
Abū Sufyān looked at it and said: “Messenger of God, you have become the richest among the Quraysh.” The Prophet smiled. Abū Sufyān continued: “Give me some of this, Messenger of God.” The Prophet turned to Bilāl who quite often acted as his treasurer and said: “Bilāl, weigh ounces of silver for Abū Sufyān and give him camels.” Abū Sufyān was gratified, but wanted to gain more. He said to the Prophet: “What about my son, Yazīd?” The Prophet said to Bilāl: “Weigh for Yazīd ounces of silver and give him camels.” Abū Sufyān then said: “And my son Muʿāwiyah, Messenger of God.” The Prophet again told Bilāl to weigh ounces of silver for Muʿāwiyah and to give him camels.
Source: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 37: Generous Gifts to Old Foes

output.scene_title: The Prophet Shows Generosity to Abū Sufyān's Sons
output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 37: Generous Gifts to Old Foes

characters.name: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
characters.appearance: Early 40s, medium build, dignified upright posture. Wearing a simple white thobe with a light brown cloak draped over shoulders, white turban with tail between shoulder blades. Simple leather sandals. Always shown from behind, face never visible. Distinguished by light brown cloak and subtle warm light.

characters.name: Abū Sufyān ibn Harb
characters.appearance: Middle-aged, dark olive skin, slightly overweight, strong build. Thick dark beard with slight grey tint, neatly kept. Wearing off-white cotton thobe, plain black woolen cloak, white cloth wrapped as loose head covering. Worn leather sandals. Confident, stern expression, prominent nose.

characters.name: Bilāl ibn Rābah
characters.appearance: Late 20s, light brown skin, lean build, tall stature. Short, neatly trimmed beard, clean-shaven face. Wearing off-white cotton thobe, plain white turban, simple leather sandals. Attentive, intelligent expression, bright eyes.

characters.name: Yazīd ibn Abū Sufyān
characters.appearance: Teenager, medium build, slightly younger than average. Short, neatly trimmed beard, clean-shaven face. Wearing off-white cotton thobe, plain white turban, simple leather sandals. Wide-eyed, enthusiastic expression, energetic posture.

characters.name: Muʿāwiyah ibn Abū Sufyān
characters.appearance: Young, dark olive skin, slender build. Short, neatly trimmed beard, clean-shaven face. Wearing off-white cotton thobe, plain white turban, simple leather sandals. Curious, alert expression, energetic posture.

output.moral_lesson: Generosity towards those who oppose you can bring peace and understanding.
output.narrative_text: Abū Sufyān asked the Prophet for some of his wealth, but the Prophet surprised him by offering gifts to his sons, Yazīd and Muʿāwiyah. Abū Sufyān was grateful but wanted more.

compliance.notes: The Prophet is shown from behind, and all characters are depicted with modest clothing. The scene is peaceful and suitable for children.

output.image_prompt: Watercolor illustration of a 7th century Makkah courtyard. A man in white thobe and light brown cloak, white turban, seen entirely from behind, standing with Bilāl, who is holding a clay pot and a balance scale. Abū Sufyān, a middle-aged man with dark olive skin, thick beard, and plain black woolen cloak, stands nearby with a look of surprise. His sons, Yazīd and Muʿāwiyah, teenagers and young boys respectively, with short beards and plain white turbans, look on with wide eyes. Simple mud-brick walls, clay water jug in corner. Medium shot, eye level. Warm golden palette. No modern objects.
